description: Edit-distance core modelled on the textdistance library: levenshtein (minimum insert/delete/substitute edit cost) and lcs (longest-common-subsequence length), with witness-minimality specs anchored on a frozen inductive Edit script and witness-maximality specs anchored on List.Sublist. Beyond the metric/recursion/concatenation laws, a hidden-invariant family states clean end-facts whose proofs each need a discovered, non-obvious invariant kept out of the specification: exact shared-prefix/shared-suffix LCS accounting, single-symbol LCS adjacency bounds, first-argument LCS monotonicity, reversal invariance of both operations (proved via an edit-script reversal construction for lev and a sublist-reverse involution for lcs), the joint lev/lcs upper bound 2*lcs + lev <= |a|+|b|, the LCS triangle inequality lcs a b + lcs b c <= |b| + lcs a c (proved via a positional-overlap interleaving lemma for two subsequences of a shared middle), general first-argument LCS Sublist-monotonicity, and the adjacent-block-swap edit-distance bound lev (a++b) (b++a) <= 2*min(|a|,|b|) (proved via an insert-block / keep / delete-block script composition), and a per-symbol counting family stating clean multiplicity end-facts: a symbol-count edit-distance lower bound, the constant-list LCS value min(count, n), the single-symbol filtered LCS min(count, count), a per-symbol LCS lower bound, and a multiset-intersection LCS upper bound. A further aligned-alignment family pins the joint structure of the two operations via constructed global witnesses: LCS superadditivity over aligned concatenation lcs a c + lcs b d <= lcs (a++b) (c++d), the shared-middle LCS lower bound |mid| + lcs a c + lcs b d <= lcs (a++mid++b) (c++mid++d), the two-order middle overlap lcs a m + lcs b m <= lcs (a++b) m + lcs (b++a) m, two-index single-position sensitivity bounds for both operations (interior delete-one-each and leading cons-pair Lipschitz laws), the cross-operation coupling lcs a c <= lev a b + lcs b c, an existential LCS-aligned edit script with cost s + 2*lcs = |a|+|b|, and the universal script lower bound max(|a|,|b|) <= lcs a b + cost s for every successful edit script. Scope: `levenshtein` models `textdistance.Levenshtein.distance` and `lcs` models `textdistance.LCSSeq.similarity` (the LCS length). Strings are modeled as `List Nat` symbol-code sequences; the normalized/ratio scores and all other textdistance algorithms are out of scope.
